sections.title: What belongs here
sections.summary: Action games, platformers, brawlers, action-adventure, character action, and other games where moment-to-moment control feel is the center of the product.

sections.title: Core structural rule
sections.summary: Protect the feel loop. Input, character state, camera, animation, collision, feedback, and encounter logic must coordinate closely, but the project should still preserve clear owne

sections.title: Preferred project structure
sections.summary: - player controller and state logic - movement/combat runtime - camera and targeting systems - animation and feedback hooks - encounter scripting or composition - content data for

sections.title: Source-of-truth boundaries
sections.summary: Source-of-truth should normally separate: - player move definitions - enemy behavior data - level or encounter data - camera and feedback tuning values The main risk is letting fee

sections.title: Verification model
sections.summary: This family needs mixed verification: - logic and state tests where possible - deterministic combat or collision tests for sensitive systems - performance profiling on hot paths -

sections.title: Release and hotfix concerns
sections.summary: Some action bugs are not just bugs, they are trust collapses. Latency spikes, unreadable hit reactions, camera fights, or collision inconsistencies can make the whole game feel wro

sections.title: Failure modes
sections.summary: - camera and control logic fighting each other - animation-driven logic with hidden gameplay consequences - unowned tuning values spread everywhere - frame-time instability in feel

sections.title: Team operating notes
sections.summary: This family needs tighter integration between design, engineering, animation, and UI than slower genres. Parallel work is still possible, but handoffs must describe how a change af

sections.title: Best Temper overlays
sections.summary: - `Miyamoto ✨` for feel and clarity - `Kaplan 🎯` for first-session trust - `Carmack 🔧` for performance and state correctness
